question: Why is multicast more bandwidth-efficient than broadcast when only a subset of receivers needs the traffic?
choice_a: Because multicast reaches only the interested group instead of every host in the broadcast domain
choice_b: Because multicast never uses IP addresses
explanation: Multicast is more efficient because it reaches only the intended group rather than every host in the broadcast domain.

question: Which statement best describes why routers separate broadcast domains?

choice_b: Routers operate at Layer 3 and do not forward Layer 2 broadcasts between subnets
choice_c: Routers replace every ARP table with a CAM table
choice_d: Routers treat every subnet as one large VLAN
explanation: Routers operate at Layer 3 and do not forward Layer 2 broadcasts between subnets.

question: Which statement best distinguishes RIP from OSPF at a high level?

choice_a: Routers can carry a smaller and cleaner view of reachable networks
choice_b: Every host gains more usable addresses

choice_d: VLAN tags are removed from trunks
